title: AN ACT REAPPORTIONING THE PROVINCE OF COTABATO INTO THREE (3) LEGISLATIVE DISTRICTS

chunks.text:
108 OG No. 45, 5780 (November 5, 2012); Philippine Star, September 18, 2012

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10177, September 14, 2012 ]

AN ACT REAPPORTIONING THE PROVINCE OF COTABATO INTO THREE (3) LEGISLATIVE DISTRICTS

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Legislative Districts.

ГўВЂВ” The Province of Cotabato, which shall be reapportioned into three (3) legislative districts effective on the next national elections after the effectivity of this Act, is hereby reapportioned as follows:

(a) First Legislative District. ГўВЂВ” The First Legislative District shall be composed of the municipalities of Pikit, Pigkawayan, Alamada, Libungan, Midsayap and Aleosan;

(b) Second Legislative District. ГўВЂВ” The Second Legislative District shall be composed of the City of Kidapawan and the municipalities of Makilala, Magpet, Roxas, Antipas and Arakan; and

(c) Third Legislative District. ГўВЂВ” The Third Legislative District shall be composed of the municipalities of Banilisan, Carmen, Kabacan, Matalam, Mlang and Tulunan.

SEC. 2.

Incumbent Representatives.

ГўВЂВ” The incumbent Representatives of the First and Second Legislative Districts of the Province of Cotabato shall continue to represent their respective legislative district until the new Representatives shall have been elected and qualified.

SEC. 3.

Rules and Regulations.

ГўВЂВ” The Commission on Elections shall issue the necessary rules and regulations to implement this Act.

SEC. 4.

Repealing Clause.

ГўВЂВ” All laws, rules and regulations which are inconsistent with this Act are hereby repealed, amended or modified accordingly.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

ГўВЂВ” This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in any newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(Sgd.) FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

(Sgd.)

JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on February 22, 2011 and July 24, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

Approved:

SEP 14 2012

(Sgd.)

BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
